summary: Added the repo-managed immutable guest replacement catalog and live-apply guard for governed services, proved the Grafana production preflight fails closed without an override, and completed a bounded monitoring replay with the documented ADR 0191 in-place exception while preserving end-to-end service health.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python -m pytest -q tests/test_immutable_guest_replacement.py tests/test_service_redundancy.py tests/test_live_apply_receipts.py` returned `16 passed`, `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/immutable_guest_replacement.py --validate` and `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate` both passed, and `python3 -m py_compile scripts/immutable_guest_replacement.py scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py` succeeded.

verification.check: Immutable replacement planner and failed-closed preflight
verification.observed: `make immutable-guest-replacement-plan service=grafana` resolved `grafana -> monitoring-lv3`, and `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/immutable_guest_replacement.py --check-live-apply --service grafana` exited `2` with `Immutable guest replacement is required before in-place live apply` until the documented override was supplied.

The official replay `make live-apply-service service=grafana env=production ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true EXTRA_ARGS='-e bypass_promotion=true'` completed cleanly from the isolated worktree with `monitoring-lv3 : ok=176 changed=0 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=34`, and the preflight logged that the governed service remained under the immutable replacement policy while the narrow override was acknowledged for this run.

`curl -Ik --resolve grafana.lv3.org:443:65.108.75.123 https://grafana.lv3.org/d/lv3-platform-overview/lv3-platform-overview` returned `HTTP/2 302` to `/login?redirectTo=%2Fd%2Flv3-platform-overview%2Flv3-platform-overview` with the expected shared edge security headers.

verification.check: Monitoring guest services and local health endpoints
verification.observed: On `monitoring-lv3`, `grafana-server.service`, `lv3-prometheus.service`, `prometheus-alertmanager.service`, `prometheus-blackbox-exporter.service`, `tempo.service`, and `otelcol-contrib.service` all remained `active`, while `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/health`, `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:9093/-/healthy`, `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:9090/-/ready`, `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:9115/-/healthy`, and `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:3200/ready` all returned healthy responses.

notes: The production replay was performed from a separate latest-main worktree on `codex/ws-0191-live-apply` so parallel agents could continue safely without sharing controller-local state.
The successful live apply intentionally used the documented ADR 0191 narrow exception for an in-place monitoring replay; this workstream does not yet implement a full guest replacement controller.

Protected integration files remain intentionally unchanged on this branch: `VERSION`, release sections in `changelog.md`, canonical observed state in `versions/stack.yaml`, and the top-level README integrated status summary.
